metadata.career_relevance: A Razorpay senior engineer reviews a pull request: 'You added AI-powered dispute classification but there are no tests. What happens when Anthropic API is down? What happens if the AI returns an unexpected format? How do I know the prompt is correct?' AI features without tests are a liability. After this topic, you can write comprehensive tests for AI features — mocking the AI, validating prompts, testing fallbacks, and ensuring the RAG pipeline retrieves the right documents.

hook.real_world_problem:
Untested AI feature in production:

```
PROBLEM:
  Payment dispute classifier deployed without tests.
  Anthropic API outage: ChatClient throws exception
  Result: entire dispute endpoint throws 500
         All merchant dispute tickets stuck
         No fallback, no graceful degradation

  Same day: AI returns unexpected format:
  Expected: '{"category": "FRAUD", "confidence": 87}'
  Got:      'Based on my analysis, this appears to be... FRAUD.'
  Result:   BeanOutputConverter.convert() throws JsonParseException
            NPE propagates to HTTP response → 500

  Root cause: no test for AI downtime, no test for format validation

WITH PROPER AI TESTING:
  @Test void apiDown_fallbackActivates():
    Stub: chatClient returns RuntimeException
    Assert: dispute classified via rule-based fallback
    Assert: HTTP 200 (not 500)

  @Test void aiReturnsWrongFormat_handledGracefully():
    Stub: chatClient returns plain text (not JSON)
    Assert: retry + fallback activated
    Assert: response still valid DisputeClassification
```
hook.what_you_will_build: Complete test suite for Razorpay's AI dispute classifier: mock responses, fallback tests, prompt validation, RAG evaluation.

visual_aid.description: Testing pyramid for AI applications. Bottom layer (most tests): Unit tests with mocked ChatClient (instant, free). Middle layer: Integration tests with WireMock stubbing Anthropic API (fast, controlled responses). Upper middle: RAG evaluation tests (InMemoryVectorStore + known test cases, measure accuracy). Top layer (fewest): End-to-end tests against real AI API (slow, expensive, non-deterministic — run nightly only). Sidebar: Fallback testing — inject AI error → verify rule-based fallback activates → assert 200 response.
visual_aid.alt_text: AI testing pyramid: unit (mock) → integration (WireMock) → RAG evaluation → E2E (real API)

worked_example.scenario: Complete test suite for Razorpay dispute classifier.
worked_example.problem_statement:
1. Mock ChatClient returns expected JSON
2. WireMock stubs Anthropic API endpoint
3. Fallback test: API throws → rule-based classifier
4. RAG evaluation: 5 test cases, measure accuracy
5. @SpringBootTest: all test types demonstrated

step_by_step_solution.details: Layer 1 (fast, free): Mock ChatClient — test your service logic without real AI calls. 95% of tests here. Layer 2 (medium): WireMock — stub the Anthropic HTTP endpoint with real JSON responses (test JSON parsing, headers). Layer 3 (expensive): Real API with temperature=0 — run only nightly in CI. Layer 4 (evaluation): RAG accuracy — measure retrieval quality on test query set. The AI is NOT what you're testing in unit tests — you're testing YOUR code's handling of AI responses.

worked_example.expected_output:
Test suite results:
  MockChatClientTest: 4 tests pass (8ms)
  FallbackTest: 2 tests pass (12ms)
  RagEvaluationTest: accuracy=80% on 10 test cases
  TotalTests: 8 pass, 0 fail, 0 ignored
things_to_notice: Mock the ChatClient, not the AI: you're testing YOUR code (error handling, JSON parsing, routing logic). The AI model is assumed correct.
JSON format test: mock returns plain text instead of JSON → BeanOutputConverter throws → your code must handle this gracefully (try-catch, fallback).
RAG accuracy target: 80%+ before deploying to production. Run the evaluation with 20+ real user queries and known correct documents.
E2E tests: expensive (pay per token). Run maximum once nightly. Mock tests: run on every PR.

industry_examples.use_case: Test pyramid: 200 unit tests (mock ChatClient, 2s total), 15 integration tests (WireMock Anthropic, 30s), 10 RAG evaluation tests (InMemoryVectorStore, 20s), 3 E2E tests (real API, 60s, nightly only). Coverage: API downtime fallback, wrong JSON format handling, low-confidence routing, RAG accuracy 85%. PR blocked if any layer fails.

applications.interview_relevance: 'How would you test an AI-powered feature?' → Testing pyramid. Unit: mock ChatClient (test service logic, error handling). Integration: WireMock stub Anthropic API (test JSON parsing, HTTP headers). Fallback: ChatClient throws → verify rule-based fallback → assert 200 HTTP. RAG: InMemoryVectorStore test cases → measure accuracy %. E2E: temperature=0, nightly only. Never test 100% against real AI — too slow, too expensive, non-deterministic.
applications.real_world_workflow: AI testing workflow: (1) unit tests with mock ChatClient for all business logic paths, (2) fallback tests: stub AI to throw → assert graceful degradation, (3) JSON format tests: stub AI to return invalid JSON → assert error handling, (4) RAG accuracy: build test set of 20+ question-answer pairs → measure top-K accuracy, (5) E2E smoke test with real API + temperature=0 (nightly), (6) CI gates: unit + integration on every PR, E2E nightly.

common_gaps.what_students_get_wrong: Only testing the happy path (AI returns correct JSON). Not testing: API timeout, wrong format, low-confidence response, rate limit exceeded, or empty context (RAG).
common_gaps.remediation: Test matrix: correct response ✓, API timeout ✓, wrong JSON format ✓, low confidence (< 70) ✓, no RAG context found ✓, rate limit error ✓. Each should have a defined behavior (fallback, error message, retry). If the behavior is undefined: define it in a test FIRST (TDD for AI).
common_gaps.detection_pattern: Test class has only one test: testClassify_happyPath() — no error path, no fallback, no format validation

key_takeaways: Mock ChatClient: test service logic (not the AI) — fast, free, deterministic
WireMock: stub Anthropic HTTP endpoint — test JSON parsing + HTTP integration
Fallback test: stub AI to throw → assert rule-based fallback activates
Format test: stub AI to return plain text → assert BeanOutputConverter handles gracefully
RAG evaluation: test question set → measure top-K accuracy → target 80%+
temperature=0: deterministic for E2E tests (same input → same output)
E2E tests: nightly only (expensive, slow, non-deterministic at temp > 0)
Test matrix: happy path + API error + wrong format + low confidence + no context
Never mock: rule-based fallback classifier (test it directly — it's pure logic)
AI prompt test: assert the prompt sent to AI contains required fields/instructions

self_check_questions.question: A developer says: 'AI tests are pointless because AI responses are non-deterministic. You can't assert the exact output.' How do you respond?
self_check_questions.answer: Partially true but misses the point. You're NOT testing the AI — you're testing YOUR CODE. What to test: (1) Fallback behavior: stub the AI to throw an exception, assert your fallback activates and returns a valid response. This is purely deterministic. (2) Format handling: stub the AI to return malformed JSON, assert your error handling catches it gracefully. (3) Routing logic: mock returns classification='FRAUD', confidence=92 → assert ticket routed to fraud team (your routing logic, not the AI). (4) RAG retrieval: InMemoryVectorStore with known documents → assert correct documents retrieved (retrieval is deterministic). Only the final E2E 'does the AI actually classify this correctly?' is non-deterministic — run those with temperature=0, limited scope, nightly. 95% of AI tests are deterministic.

reflection.next_topic_preview: Module 26 is complete! You now have the full AI-powered Java toolkit: Spring AI, RAG, AI features (extraction/classification/summarization), LangChain4j, and comprehensive testing strategies for AI applications.
